data.title: GLP-1 trials show mood improvement rather than the ‘Ozempic personality’ – Dr. Angela Fitch
data.description:
Data from randomized controlled trials shows that most patients’ moods actually improve when treated for obesity with GLP-1 medications, according to Dr. Angela Fitch, Knownwell chief medical officer and president of the Obesity Medicine Association.

“The overwhelming data has shown, in randomized controlled trials at least, that most patients’ mood gets better with treatment of their obesity,” Dr. Fitch said in an interview with CNBC.

Responding to a Washington Post story about so-called “Ozempic personality” and social media anecdotes describing emotional flatness, Dr. Fitch said her clinic has not observed these trends.

At Knownwell, where she oversees treatment of more than 20,000 patients across the country, Dr. Fitch noted they closely monitor mood but have not seen an increase in the issues described in recent media reports.

The evidence regarding severe mental health risks has been equally reassuring. Multiple organizations worldwide have examined data specifically related to mood changes and suicidality and found no increase in these concerns. Dr. Fitch noted that the lack of evidence led regulators to recently remove the suicidality warning from the medication labels.

Despite these findings, Dr. Fitch emphasized that patients should not obtain GLP-1 prescriptions “without the comprehensive wraparound care from a clinician that knows what they’re doing and how to help.”

She stressed that weight management has always had the potential to affect mood in various ways, making proper clinical oversight essential regardless of the treatment approach.

When asked whether mental health concerns might limit the GLP-1 market, Dr. Fitch said the patient population qualifying for these medications remains vast. She clarified that gastrointestinal side effects, not mood changes, remain the primary reason patients discontinue treatment.

For patients experiencing feelings of being “meh,” Dr. Fitch recommended a multifaceted approach beyond medication alone.

She pointed to counseling, behavioral management, and increased physical activity as important tools for addressing mood shifts, noting that treating weight requires comprehensive care that accounts for changes in dopamine-related reward systems.

Pharmaceuticals associated with GLP-1: (LLY), (NVO), (VKTX), (GPCP), (AMGN), (PFE), (HIMS)

GLP-1 investment fund ETFs: (OZEM), (HRTS), (THNR)

data.title: Scotiabank expects ~$56M contribution from KeyCorp's Q1 earnings
data.description:
Scotiabank (BNS) expects the net income contribution from its ownership interest in KeyCorp (KEY) to be ~C$77M (US$56M) in its Q2 2026 results, the Canadian bank said Friday.

The contribution represents KeyCorp's Q1 net income, includes acquisition-related and other accounting impacts, is net of the bank's  associated funding costs, and is reported on a one-month lag. Scotiabank will release its earnings for its fiscal Q2 2026, which ends April 20, 2026, on May 27.

Adjusting for the amortization of acquired intangible assets of ~C$8M, Scotiabank's adjusted net income contribution from Key will be ~C$85M, it added.

The Canadian bank acquired a 14.9% stake in KeyCorp (KEY) in late 2024, giving Key the opportunity to reposition its balance sheet as higher interest rates had reduced the value of some securities it held.

data.title: STMicroelectronics, TXN get upgrades, while NXP Semiconductors cut at Mizuho
data.description:
Mizuho upgraded ratings of STMicroelectronics (STM) and Texas Instruments (TXN), while downgrading NXP Semiconductors (NXPI).

The firm upgraded STMicroelectronics to Outperform from Neutral and raised the price target to $48 from $32. Texas Instruments was upped to Neutral and Underperform, and its price target was increased to $215 from $160. NXP Semiconductors was cut to Underperform from Outperform, and its price target was reduced to $188 from $255. In addition, Mizuho kept Mobileye's Global (MBLY) rating at Neutral but cut the price target to $8 from $11.

STMicroelectronics (STM) and Texas Instruments (TXN)

Analysts led by Vijay Rakesh see STMicroelectronics benefitting from tailwinds in AI server power management and silicon photonics, or SiPho, ramps, while also noting tailwinds from physical AI over the long term with self-driving and humanoid robots and increasing Amazon's LEO satellite deployments. The analysts noted that the increasing demand for LEO satellites is an additional tailwind, helping offset some headwinds in auto/auto in the near term as macro/memory limits vehicle/handset sales in 2026.

"Broad China Price Increases and AI DC [data center] Tailwinds L-T [long term] as Analog Suppliers Look to 800V AI Server Architectures (Positive TXN and STM). We believe strong AI server and Industrial demand is driving TXN and other analog suppliers to raise prices in China 10%+ in April (a big positive driving JunQ upside). We see NVDA GPU and Custom ASIC [application-specific integrated circuit] push to 800V/48V/12V DC AI server architectures driving UPSIDE to power analog demand for TXN/STM," said Rakesh and his team.

In addition, the analysts believe that Texas Instruments and STMicroelectronics are beneficiaries of an Industrials recovery, with: more than 20% revenue in Industrials; exposed to key markets in aerospace and defense, or A&D, medical, and clean energy; and recent segment-specific price hikes of over 10% for stock-keeping units, or SKUs, in power semis for AI server driving upside.

The analysts said that they have seen gradual global manufacturing improvement since the start of 2025 into 2026, as the EU/U.S./China Purchasing Managers Index, or PMI, is now all more than 50 (expansion territory) in 2026 after 2023-2025 saw headwinds.

"We see core industrial demand momentum accelerating into 2H26E, with 1H26E still potentially slightly muted, among reshoring and reindustrializing efforts across N.Am. [North America] and Europe, driving analog from inventory digestion to pockets of improvement," said Rakesh and his team.

NXP Semiconductors (NXPI)

"We believe NXPI saw strong tailwinds for its auto business in 2021-22 following the Covid downturn when it benefited from customers with low inventory and high pricing, and outperformed LVP [Light Vehicle Production]. However, in 2023-25 post-Covid recovery, we saw NXPI auto revenue underperform LVP growth y/y, with customers now sitting on elevated inventories and auto demand fairly muted with growth slowing outside of BEVs. We see headwinds from the memory shortage, as well as ongoing macro problems from raised ASPs and the Iran war, weighing on the auto sector in 2026E, potentially limiting NXPI's revenue growth as autos makes up ~55-60% of its revenue mix," said Rakesh and his team.

The analysts added that NXP has noted tailwinds from AI at the edge, mainly in factory automation and power management, but they believe NXP's AI DC exposure remains limited versus peers, potentially limiting long-term upside from a key analog driver moving forward.

data.title: Badger Meter GAAP EPS of $0.93 misses by $0.26, revenue of $202.3M misses by $28.58M
data.description:
• Badger Meter press release (BMI): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.93 misses by $0.26.
  • Revenue of $202.3M (-9.0% Y/Y) misses by $28.58M.

  • Utility water sales declined 10% year-over-year, reflecting project timing and softer short‑cycle municipal customer ordering, partially offset by SaaS, SmartCover, water quality and network monitoring performance.

     

  • Sales of flow instrumentation products decreased 4%, with modest growth in water-related markets more than offset by lower demand in the de-emphasized array of market applications.

  • Shares -18%.

data.title: Repay stock jumps on $4.80 apiece acquisition proposal by largest stockholder
data.description:
Repay's (RPAY) largest shareholder, Forager Capital Management, has proposed to acquire the remaining shares of the company in a $4.80 per share all-cash transaction.

Forager currently owns ~13% of Repay's issued and outstanding shares.

Shares were over 30% higher to $4.14 after the market opened on Friday.

The proposed acquisition consideration represents a 75% premium to the company's 30-day volume-weighted average price of $2.75.

"We have access to sufficient financing to complete the transaction," said the shareholder letter by Johnny Wilhelm, partner of Forager.

data.title: Dollar reversal deepens as Hormuz reopens and safe-haven trade unwinds
data.description:
The U.S. dollar (DXY) retreated sharply on Friday, erasing its recent Middle East conflict-driven gains as easing geopolitical tensions reduced demand for safe-haven assets.

The move followed confirmation from Iran that the Strait of Hormuz remains fully open to commercial shipping during the ongoing ceasefire, a development that has helped stabilize global energy markets and calm investor nerves.

The DXY fell 0.4% to 97.75 after briefly touching an intraday low of 97.63—its weakest level since late February. The decline marks a notable reversal from earlier in the conflict, when the index surged to as high as 100.64 amid heightened uncertainty and rising oil prices.  Since then, the dollar has dropped roughly 2.8%, reflecting a broader shift in market sentiment.

Investors have increasingly rotated into alternative currencies as crude prices eased and expectations grew for potential diplomatic engagement between Washington and Tehran. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi indicated that shipping activity through the critical waterway would continue uninterrupted for the duration of the ceasefire, reinforcing optimism around de-escalation. 

From a technical standpoint, the dollar remains under pressure. The index is now trading below its 50-day, 100-day, and 200-day moving averages, while its relative strength index sits near 35.7, approaching—but not yet reaching—oversold territory. 

U.S. Dollar ETFs: (UUP), (USDU), and (UDN). 

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: U.S. stocks rise to new highs as the Strait of Hormuz is declared open
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages jumped on Friday to record highs after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz open for commercial vessels. President Donald Trump, however, said the naval blockade will remain in effect.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.9%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +1%, and the Dow (DJI) was +1.5%.

The Ports and Maritime Organization of the Islamic Republic of Iran said the Strait of Hormuz is now “completely open” for all commercial vessels for the rest of the Lebanon ceasefire timeline. This comes after Israel and Lebanon announced a 10-day ceasefire on Thursday.

Nonetheless, Trump said that the naval blockade on Iranian ports will remain in full force and effect. The reopening of the passageway was one of the conditions of the fragile two-week ceasefire that is currently in effect.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) rapidly decreased to $85, while Brent (CO1:COM) also dropped to $89 per barrel.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 7 basis points lower at 4.25%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 7 basis points to 3.72%.

“The reported reopening of the Strait of Hormuz by Iran is undoubtedly a welcome relief for the global economy. Needless to say, its true impact depends on the durability of this reopening, which will require sustained confidence-building measures from all three warring parties,” said Mohamed El-Erian, chief economic adviser at Allianz.

“Equally critical is the reaction of ship operators. While their immediate priority is to bring out vessels currently in the region as soon as they can, the real litmus test lies in their willingness to send new ships in. Their return is necessary for restoring the global energy flow and bringing regional production sites fully back online,” El-Erian added.

data.title: OraSure gains after cooperation pact with activist, adds nominee to board
data.description:
OraSure (OSUR) ticked higher by 0.3% after entering a cooperation pact with activist Altai Capital and naming John D. Bertrand to its board.

As part of the agreement, Altai will withdraw its director nominations for the annual meeting, and members of OraSure’s board and management team will meet with Altai on a regular basis to discuss financial and strategic matters, according to a statement on Thursday.

OraSure (OSUR) also announced it will seek shareholder approval to declassify its board at the 2026 annual meeting. 

“We are pleased with the outcome of our engagement with OraSure and the steps the Company has taken to enhance its Board and governance practices,” said Rishi Bajaj, Founder and CIO of Altai. “John’s appointment, together with our ongoing constructive dialogue with OraSure’s Board and management team, strengthens our confidence that the Company is on the path to delivering sustained value.

The cooperation pact comes after Altai last month reiterated its call for Bajaj and Digital Diagnostics CEO Bertrand to be added to the board. The activist was pushing OraSure (OSUR) to pursue a potential sale that could result in the doubling of the medical device maker's share price.

data.title: Compass to become 51% owner in undisclosed company
data.description:
Compass (COMP) has agreed to become the 51% holder of a company's common equity, entering into a 30-month installment payment plan with the undisclosed entity.

The real estate services provider revealed a multi-party transaction with the company, which is set to indirectly own certain franchisees operating under the Sotheby's International Realty brand.

data.title: U.S.–Iran nuclear deal odds climb fast as July odds approach 70%
data.description:
Negotiations between the United States and Iran appear to be gaining traction, with early-stage discussions reportedly focused on a framework aimed at reducing regional tensions. According to Axios, officials from both sides are reviewing a concise three-page proposal that outlines potential terms for de-escalation.

Central to the talks is a possible agreement in which Iran would relinquish its stockpile of enriched uranium in exchange for access to roughly $20B in previously frozen financial assets. While the discussions remain preliminary, the structure of the proposal signals a shift toward tangible concessions on both sides. 

Market participants are increasingly factoring in the likelihood of a breakthrough. Data from prediction platforms indicates traders are assigning approximately a 70% probability that a nuclear agreement could be reached by early July. The sharp rise in expectations underscores growing confidence that diplomatic momentum may translate into a near-term deal, though key geopolitical and verification challenges remain unresolved. 

Outlined below are the priced-in forecasts by Kalshi traders when asked, When will a U.S.–Iran nuclear deal take place?

  • Before May — 34% chance.
  • Before June — 54% chance.
  • Before July — 69% chance. 
  • Before August — 64% chance.
  • Before September — 65% chance.
  • Before 2027 — 74% chance.
  • Before 2028 — 82% chance. 
  • Before Jan 20, 2029 — 80% chance.

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).
